paper_title: Contact mechanics for randomly rough surfaces
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/11_Contact mechanics for randomly rough surfaces.pdf
sections_checked: 3.3 The theory of Bush, Gibson and Thomas
3.7 On the validity of contact mechanics theories
5 Elastic contact mechanics with graded elasticity
8.6 Contact mechanics and adhesion for viscoelastic solids
8.7 Strong adhesion for elastically stiff materials
11 On the philosophy of contact mechanics
12 Comment on numerical studies of contact mechanics
Appendix A: Power spectrum of frozen capillary waves
Appendix B: Stress distribution in adhesive contact between smooth spherical surfaces

equations_checked.equation: Unnumbered PSD definition and self-affine scaling
equations_checked.notes: Retained as E1. Fourier normalization, height correlation, isotropy statement, exponent -2(H+1), Df=3-H and qL/q0/q1 meanings were visually checked on 300 dpi renders.

Retained as E2. Modal amplitude (2pi/L)sqrt(C), independent phase interval [0,2pi), and L=sqrt(A0) were visually checked. The paper does not explicitly state the conjugate-symmetry implementation for a real height field.
Frozen-capillary-wave spectrum and rms formula were checked but omitted because PMMA/Pyrex preparation is not the target red-brick topography model.
Hertz and Greenwood-Williamson formulas were checked. They are background single-scale asperity models and are superseded here by the multiscale equations retained as E3-E6.
Bush low-load slope was checked; the common form and both kappa values are retained through Eq. (22) as E6.
Retained as E3. Contact-only integration domain, omission of the non-contact delta mass, projected area, and units of P were checked.
Retained as E4. Diffusion equation, squared elastic factor, qL*q^3*C(q), q=zeta*qL and delta initial condition were visually checked on a 400 dpi render.
Retained as E5. The upper integral limit and error-function argument were verified as 1/sqrt(G) and 1/(2sqrt(G)); the text-layer extraction alone was ambiguous, so the 400 dpi page render was used.
Retained as E6. Integral measure d^2q, q^2*C(q), exponent -1/2 and kappa values sqrt(2pi) versus sqrt(8/pi) were visually checked.

Hertz pressure example, load first-moment conservation, P(0,zeta)=0 proof and non-contact area flux were checked on a 300 dpi render. Intermediate derivation was omitted; the boundary result is retained in M3/E4.
Retained selectively as E7. Both absorbing boundaries, plastic-area flux sign, constant-hardness assumption and area/load conservation were visually checked.
Retained as E8. Both sigmaY-prime terms, derivative sign and moving-boundary condition were checked on the rendered page.

Adhesive, graded-elasticity, viscoelastic and combined elastoplastic-adhesive equations were reviewed but omitted from the minimal card because the target steel-spine/red-brick model is non-adhesive and brittle-damage dominated.
Pyrex ball-on-flat adhesive pull-off and loading-cycle relations were checked but are application-specific and not transferable to the target contact.
Capillary-wave derivation and adhesive spherical stress distribution were checked; they do not add target-specific mechanism beyond the retained PSD and non-adhesive boundary equations.

figures_checked.reason: Introductory real-versus-nominal contact schematic; compactly stated in text.
qL/q0/q1 and spectral slope are fully retained by E1 and the parameter table.
Asphalt-specific PSD example; the negative conclusion is retained without the plot.
Asphalt height histograms are not target parameters.
PMMA-versus-steel spectral comparison is summarized as a scale-weighting warning.
Smooth-sphere Hertz geometry is background and not the finite-spine geometry.
Single-scale Greenwood-Williamson schematic is superseded by the retained multiscale model.
Greenwood-Williamson numerical example is not needed for the selected mechanism chain.
Irreplaceable conceptual map from magnification to hidden partial contact; crop includes all scale labels and the full caption.
Contact-area curves are represented analytically by E5 and summarized by E6.
Numerical kappa comparison is retained quantitatively in V1-V2.
Contact-island hierarchy is conceptually covered by extracted Fig. 9.
Macroasperity definition is not needed for the target minimal model.
Biological graded-adhesion example is outside the non-adhesive target branch.
Minimal direct evidence for elastic/plastic/non-contact area evolution with scale and hardness; crop includes both panels, axes, labels and full caption.
Adhesive detachment scaling schematic is outside scope.
Adhesive crack-tip stress schematic is outside scope.
Rubber adhesion parameter study is not transferable to steel-spine/red-brick contact.
Adhesive contact area/effective energy versus scale is outside scope.
Adhesion-versus-no-adhesion rubber result is outside scope.
Viscoelastic adhesive crack process zone is outside scope.
Biological terminal-element montage is background only.
PMMA shear/contact comparison is application-specific and not a target parameter source.
Pyrex pull-off data concern adhesion after plastic smoothing, not microspine interlock.
Pyrex capillary-wave spectrum is material-specific.
Pyrex adhesion/contact curves are outside the selected non-adhesive branch.
Pyrex elastoplastic example is more application-specific than retained Fig. 15.
Adhesive roughness-barrier mechanism is outside scope.
Continuum-to-atomistic handoff schematic is adequately stated in the limitations.
Irreplaceable visual link between the P(0)=0 boundary, sparse-grid error and same-load contact-area overestimate; crop includes axes and full caption.
Adhesive spherical stress distribution is outside the target branch.

omitted_sections: Detailed Hertz and Greenwood-Williamson background algebra
Frozen-capillary-wave material examples beyond the PSD warning
Graded elasticity and biological adhesion examples
PMMA, Pyrex, tire-rim and pull-off application parameters

uncertain_items.detail: The printed complex exponential sum does not explicitly state the Hermitian pairing of positive and negative wavevectors. A real implementation must add this condition and verify the discrete PSD normalization.

The local PDF identifies itself as arXiv v1 and does not print the DOI. The DOI was taken from the project core-literature registry; all page and equation locations in this extraction refer only to the local preprint.

known_warnings: The main PSD/contact equations assume statistical isotropy; directional brick texture requires C(qx,qy) or an explicitly directional representation.
The elastic equations are written for an elastic body against a rigid rough substrate; a two-material equivalent modulus is required for steel-spine/red-brick contact.
The ideal-plastic hardness boundary is not a brittle fracture, crushing or debris model and must not replace red-brick damage evidence.
Eq. (20) was verified visually because text extraction obscured the reciprocal square-root in the upper limit and error-function argument.
The 22% contact-area bias in Fig. 30 is illustrative, not a universal correction factor.
The 2006 discussion of computationally accessible scale decades is historical; retain the convergence principle, not the dated hardware limit.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains four independent retained submodels.

render_review.all_pages: All 29 pages rendered at 120 dpi and visually inspected in eight contact sheets.
render_review.formula_pages: Pages 2, 3, 7, 8, 11, 13 and 26 were additionally inspected at 300-400 dpi.
render_review.font_warnings: Poppler reported missing display fonts Symbol/ArialUnicode on some pages, but visual inspection confirmed retained symbols and equations rendered legibly.
